title: Bridge Paper: What fMRI studies say about the nature of the psychedelic effect: a scoping review.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.
**Phase context:** psychedelic, psilocybin, DMT, ego dissolution, ayahuasca, altered states, default mode network

Paper: What fMRI studies say about the nature of the psychedelic effect: a scoping review.
DOI: 10.3389/fnins.2025.1606798
Abstract: Research on psychedelic drugs, such as psilocybin, LSD or DMT, is a burgeoning field, with an increasing number of studies showing their promise in treatment of mental disorders as well as examining their mechanism of action. Determining their effect on the brain is crucial from clinical standpoint, but also offers highly promising avenues of advancement in basic neuroscience-functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) is one of the most useful techniques to do so, with a number of newly publis

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-11. Phase 14 (Nonordinary Rendering).
